content.full_text:
State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 807 · SACRAMENTO, 95804 · · · 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814

Technical Assistance · · Administration · · Executive/Legal · · Enforcement · · Statement of Economic Interest
(916) 322-5662       322-5660         322-5901         322-6441         322-6444

December 30, 1981


Honorable Richard A. Adler
Judge of the Municipal Court
1633 Purdue Avenue
Los Angeles, CA 90025

Dear Judge Adler:

     Thank you for your telephone call requesting advice
from this office. I apologize for the delay in my written
response to your inquiry. You asked whether you have to
report interest you receive from an Investment Annuity.
This Annuity was established when you purchased bonds regis-
tered with the Securities and Exchange Commission and tendered
them to an insurance company which made the necessary arrange-
ments for the Annuity. A bank holds the bonds for the insurance
company. You recently withdrew a portion of the interest
generated by the bonds, and your specific question is whether
this interest must be reported as "income" on your Statement
of Economic Interests.

     The interest payments on your Investment Annuity are
not required to be reported because, as interest on bonds
registered with the SEC, they are exempt from the Act's
definition of "income." Government Code Section 83020(b)(5)1/
states:

          (b)   "Income" ... does not include:

               (5)  Dividends, interest or any other return
          on a security which is registered with the Securities
          and Exchange Commission of the United States government.

     If you have any additional questions, please feel free
to contact me at 916/322-5901.

                                        Very truly yours,

                                        [signature]
                                        Janis Shank McLean
                                        Counsel
                                        Legal Division

JSM:km

This Annuity was established when you purchased bonds registered with the Securities and Exchange Commission and tendered them to an insurance company which made the necessary arrangements for the Annuity. A bank holds the bonds for the insurance company. You recently withdrew a portion of the interest generated by the bonds, and your specific question is whether this interest must be reported as "income" on your Statement of Economic Interests.
sections.conclusion: The interest payments on your Investment Annuity are not required to be reported because, as interest on bonds registered with the SEC, they are exempt from the Act's definition of "income." Government Code Section 83020(b)(5) states: (b) "Income" ... does not include: (5) Dividends, interest or any other return on a security which is registered with the Securities and Exchange Commission of the United States government.
sections.question_synthetic: Must interest received from an investment annuity established through SEC-registered bonds be reported as income on a Statement of Economic Interests?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: Interest payments on the investment annuity are not required to be reported because they are exempt from the Political Reform Act's definition of income under Government Code Section 83020(b)(5), which excludes interest on securities registered with the SEC.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Standard advice letter format with clear question and conclusion sections. Document dated December 30, 1981. Addressed to judicial officer. Good extraction confidence; minor OCR artifacts present (e.g., 'CalifoTllia') but content is clear.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: You asked whether you have to report interest you receive from an Investment Annuity. This Annuity was established when you purchased bonds registered with the Securities and Exchange Commission and tendered them to an insurance company which made the necessary arrangements for the Annuity. A bank holds the bonds for the insurance company. You recently withdrew a portion of the interest generated by the bonds, and your specific question is whether this interest must be reported as "income" on your Statement of Economic Interests.

CONCLUSION: The interest payments on your Investment Annuity are not required to be reported because, as interest on bonds registered with the SEC, they are exempt from the Act's definition of "income." Government Code Section 83020(b)(5) states: (b) "Income" ... does not include: (5) Dividends, interest or any other return on a security which is registered with the Securities and Exchange Commission of the United States government.

Adler Judge of the Municipal Court 1633 Purdue Avenue Los Angeles, CA 90025 Dear Judge Adler: Thank you for your telephone call requesting advice from this office. I apologize for the delay in my written response to your inquiry. You asked whether you have to report interest you receive from an Investment Annuity. This Annuity was established when you purchased bonds regis- tered with the Securities and Exchange Commission and tendered them to an insurance company which made the necessary arrange- ments for the Annuity. A bank holds the bonds for the insurance company. You recently withdrew a portion of the interest generated by the bonds, and your specific question is whether this interest must be reported as "income" on your Statement of Economic Interests. The interest payments on your Investment Annuity are not required to be reported because, as interest on bonds registered with the SEC, they are exempt from the Act's definition of "income." Government Code Section 83020(b)(5)1/ states: (b) "Income" ... does not include: (5) Dividends, interest or any other return on a security which is registered with the Securities and Exchange Commission of the United States government. If you have any additional questions, please feel free to contact me at 916/322-5901. Very truly yours, [signature] Janis Shank McLean Counsel Legal Division JSM:km
embedding.summary: FPPC Counsel advises Judge Richard A. Adler that interest received from an investment annuity based on SEC-registered bonds is exempt from reporting requirements under the Political Reform Act as it falls within the statutory exemption for interest on SEC-registered securities.
